evals.prompt: You are reviewing the wave 2 diff for project test-benchmark (OAuth2 authentication feature). The diff is at .ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/sample-diff.patch. Project conventions are in .ai/projects/test-benchmark/knowledge/conventions.yml and design tokens in .ai/projects/test-benchmark/design/tokens/. The dev config is at .ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/dev-config.yml. Review all changed files for code quality, pattern adherence, security (OWASP top 10), and design token compliance. Write findings to .ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/code-review-output.yml with severity levels.
evals.expected_output: A structured review with findings covering hardcoded colors (#1a1a2e, red, #ccc, #f5f5f5, #ddd), convention violations, and security observations. Each finding should have file, severity, and message.
expectations: Identifies hardcoded color values in LoginPage.tsx (style={{ color: '#1a1a2e' }}) as a design token violation
Identifies hardcoded color 'red' in LoginPage.tsx error message as a design token violation
Identifies hardcoded colors/spacing in OAuthButton.tsx (#f5f5f5, #ddd, 12px 24px) as design token violations
Identifies hardcoded #ccc border in RoleGuard.tsx as a design token violation
Rates design token violations as warning severity (not critical)
Output contains structured findings with file path, severity level, and descriptive message for each issue

evals.prompt: Review the RBAC middleware at src/middleware/rbac.ts from the test-benchmark project wave 2 diff (.ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/sample-diff.patch). Focus specifically on security review — check for OWASP top 10 issues. The auth middleware is also in the diff. Project state is at .ai/projects/test-benchmark/state.yml.
evals.expected_output: Security-focused review of the RBAC and auth middleware, checking for proper authentication, authorization patterns, and common security pitfalls.
expectations: Checks that authentication is verified before authorization in the RBAC middleware
Notes that the middleware returns null to proceed, which is an unusual pattern worth documenting
Does not flag false positive security issues that aren't actually present in the code

evals.prompt: Review the wave 2 diff (.ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/sample-diff.patch) for convention compliance against the project conventions in .ai/projects/test-benchmark/knowledge/conventions.yml. Check naming conventions, export patterns, and structural patterns. Dev config is at .ai/projects/test-benchmark/dev/dev-config.yml.
evals.expected_output: Convention compliance review checking PascalCase components, named exports, file naming, and pattern adherence.
expectations: Confirms that components use PascalCase naming (LoginPage, OAuthButton, RoleGuard)
Confirms that all exports are named exports (no default exports)
Checks that utility functions use camelCase (hasPermission, verifyToken)
Checks that constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (ROLE_PERMISSIONS, PROVIDER_CONFIG)
